sourceMovie.title: High Fidelity
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: High Fidelity works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The High Note
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If High Fidelity clicked for you, The High Note (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after High Fidelity?
faq.answer: Try The High Note, The Lobster, About a Boy—each shares DNA with High Fidelity while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is High Fidelity?
faq.answer: High Fidelity (2000) is best described as Drama, Comedy, Romance, Music. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is High Fidelity worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
